items.title: 'Woltemade weer gelinkt aan Bayern, Rüdiger kan naar Engeland'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Can you name every team to finish top or bottom in Premier League?
items.summary: We want you to name every team who has finished top or bottom in the Premier League era. Good luck!

items.title: Feyenoord biedt kijkje achter de schermen bij 'first day' Sterling
items.summary: Feyenoord heeft via de officiële kanalen een filmpje gedeeld van de eerste trainingsdag van Raheem Sterling. De Rotterdammers zijn naar België afgereisd, zodat de Engelsman, die nog geen werkvergunning heeft in Nederland, daar wel vast aan de slag mag gaan met zijn nieuwe teamgenoten.

items.title: Lof voor Lang en Makkelie in Turkse media na 'gigantisch pak slaag' voor Juve
items.summary: Turkse media zijn lyrisch na de eclatante overwinning van Galatasaray op Juventus. Na het heenduel in de tussenronde van de Champions League worden de Turkse club, Noa Lang én Danny Makkelie bedolven onder de complimenten.

items.title: De scheefstand in de Eredivisie is eindelijk voorbij
items.summary: Met het uitspelen van Sparta Rotterdam – NEC gisteravond is de stand in de Eredivisie weer helemaal gelijk. En dat is goed nieuws voor iedereen met dezelfde afwijking als ondergetekende.

items.title: 'Laat Vinicius daar een dansje doen, daar is niks fouts aan'
items.summary: Vinicius Junior haalde de woede van de Benfica-spelers en -fans op de hals door zijn wonderschone doelpunt op provocerende wijze te vieren. In de ZSM van vanochtend spreekt Rob Aarts zich uit over de situatie en de ophef na de treffer van de aanvaller van Real Madrid.

items.title: Vini Jr. accuses 'coward' Prestianni of racist abuse
items.summary: Real Madrid forward Vinícius Júnior accused Benfica's Gianluca Prestianni of racially abusing him during the teams' Champions League playoff first leg on Tuesday.

items.title: Benfica: Vini Jr. teammates 'couldn't have heard'
items.summary: Benfica have denied that Real Madrid players could have heard the alleged racist abuse towards Vinícius Júnior.

items.title: Mourinho critical of Vinícius: 'Celebrate like Pele'
items.summary: José Mourinho criticized Vinícius Júnior's goal celebration after the forward scored in Real Madrid's 1-0 Champions League win at Benfica, before a confrontation in which the player said he was racially abused.

items.title: Balogun double sets U.S. mark in UCL knockouts
items.summary: American forward Folarin Balogun took less than one minute to give Monaco the lead against Paris Saint-Germain in their Champions League playoff on Tuesday.

items.title: 'Cowards' - Vinicius speaks out as Benfica deny racism claims
items.summary: The Champions League match between Real Madrid and Benfica was halted for 10 minutes, with both sets of players leaving the pitch.

items.title: Howe wants players to 'step up' - is this Ramsey's time to shine?
items.summary: Eddie Howe has called on his Newcastle players to continue to "step up" in Bruno Guimaraes' absence - is this Jacob Ramsey's time to shine?

items.title: Fooled once but not twice, Real Madrid learned the right lessons
items.summary: Three weeks after Benfica's shocking win over Real Madrid, lightning couldn't strike twice.

items.title: African football chief ‘occupying seat illegally’ and must go, says leading executive
items.summary: Samir Sobha says Caf’s statutes are not being respected Véron Mosengo-Omba is past compulsory retirement age A member of the Confederation of African football’s executive committee has said the general secretary, Véron Mosengo-Omba, is “occupying the seat illegally” and must be made to stand down. Samir Sobha, the president of the Mauritius Football Association, said he would not accept Mosengo-Omba’s presence at Caf meetings because the 66-year-old Swiss-Congolese lawyer no longer holds the position legitimately. Continue reading...

items.title: ‘Different but the same’: how Arsenal are keeping disabled fans in the game
items.summary: In tandem with Game Day Vision, the Premier League club are improving the matchday experience for supporters with a variety of conditions Thomas Clements’ eyes begin dancing as he recalls in vivid detail his first trip to Highbury. It was 1995 and Ian Wright was among the scorers as QPR were defeated. Clements – named after Mickey Thomas, scorer of Arsenal’s decisive second goal against Liverpool in their 1989 title win – points to his dad, Kevin, standing a metre away. “I was sat on his shoulders in the North Bank,” he says. That is, in itself, not unusual for a child of the 1980s. However, whereas most regular match-goers might take for granted the seemingly small things – travel arrangements, the journey to the stadium, grabbing food and drink, meeting friends and family, entering and exiting the ground – for disabled supporters such as Clements, careful thought and planning go into all arrangements. Continue reading...

items.title: Has a footballer ever been sent off but still named player of the match? | The Knowledge
items.summary: Plus: high-scoring symmetrical scorelines, Scottish two-club title winners and an almost-one-club manager Mail us with your questions and answers “Has a footballer ever been sent off but still won player of the match?” asked Jimmy Clark. The short answer is yes, quite a few. We’ll kick off with a couple of recent examples. “In 2024 Anthony Gordon was shown a second yellow card for Newcastle against West Ham just as the TNT commentary team were declaring him the player of the match,” writes Tom Reed. You can see the moment in question in this video (around 2:50), as Gordon is dismissed after kicking the ball away. Perhaps the substitute Harvey Barnes, who scored twice in the 4-3 comeback win, would have been a better choice. Continue reading...

items.title: Is VAR any better in the rest of Europe's top leagues?
items.summary: You do not need to look far to find people proclaiming that VAR works fine outside the Premier League. BBC Sport spoke to people across Europe's top leagues to find out.

items.title: Big Cadillac returns to calmer waters at Southwell
items.summary: We have jumps action from Southwell on Wednesday, live on Sky Sports Racing...

items.title: F1 Testing: Ferrari impressing again as Red Bull suffer issue LIVE!
Vinícius Júnior fury after alleged racist abuse mars Real Madrid’s win at Benfica
items.summary: Vinícius Júnior and his teammates appeared ready to walk off and the game was delayed for 10 minutes as the Brazilian was allegedly subject to racist abuse after scoring the goal that gives Real Madrid a 1-0 first-leg lead in their Champions League playoff against Benfica. The forward had celebrated a sensational strike by dancing in the corner of the Estádio da Luz and in the confrontations that followed, Benfica’s Gianluca Prestianni said something to him while covering his mouth. Vinícius immediately ran to the referee, François Letexier, who stopped the match and crossed his arms to signal that he was activating the anti-racism protocol. Continue reading...
